title: Taeniosapium goudotianum
intro: Taeniosapium goudotianum, also known as the Goudot's sapium, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Taeniosapium goudotianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Taeniosapium goudotianum is commonly prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Taeniosapium goudotianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Taeniosapium, which is a group of plants that are native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is often classified as a type of citrus relative due to its similarities with the Rutaceae family.
